title: lissr 1.1.0 patch notes
text: C1, snapshot recode semantics. value_recode, recode_to_na, recode branch build every mask column stood rule started, overlapping maps chain. Evidence: replaying old sequential loop real distributions misclassifies 86/2,992 answered respondents (2.9 percent) cr08a 312/2,222 (14.0 percent) cr14g; patched engine end--end cr merge reproduces verified era-1 era-2 target distributions exactly (harness B1, regression test “cr three-era harmonization”). Fix ordering mattered: engine snapshot landed cr recipe keys, fixing keys alone converted silent omission silent corruption. C8/H1, superseded releases file discovery. discover_wave_files now separates recipe-declared aux_files primary candidates, resolves aux declarations independently file_pattern (narrowed pattern silently drop declaration), ranks release versions several primaries match (keep highest, warn ignored files; abort unrankable), merge_liss_module enforces aux rows disjoint primary id variable, plus unconditional duplicate-id gate per wave. Evidence: cd10c releases disk shipped engine produced 7,252 rows wave cd10c resurrected redacted cd10c059; patched merge yields 3,626 unique respondents, s059, warning naming ignored 1.0p file pattern matches (harness A5/A6, B2). fallback pattern restricted data extensions codebook PDFs can never swept , read_wave_file aborts unknown extensions instead parsing CSV (H2). Read semantics (new finding patch work). haven’s default user_na = FALSE converts SPSS user-defined missing codes NA read; 1.0.0 engine used default, declared DK/refusal codes silently masked recipes ran (unlogged, DK collapsed item nonresponse, undeclared sentinels missed). 1.1.0 engine reads user_na = TRUE recipes receive codes convert auditably. corrects framing earlier revision verification report; report now carries corrected methodological note. C9, label user-missing round-trip. labelled_policy: to_numeric engine stashes value labels, na_values, na_range, variable label per wave read, write restores haven::labelled_spss provably safe: identical metadata across contributing waves every observed value accounted label, declared missing code, NA. Non-restorable columns pass residual sweep converts codes NA per wave (code declared missing wave never swept wave B) honors recipe exclude blocks veto. Restoration coerces string-typed metadata losslessly wrapped metadata quirk can abort phase 7 (real cr string variable na_values “999” exercised ). Evidence: cv verification merge writes 75 labelled columns, LABEL_RESTORE NA_SWEEP appear JSONL log, cv20l’s excluded suffix 243 keeps 154 eligibility -9s -9 survives outside carve-outs (harness B5/B6). C6, validation honesty strict gate. Unimplemented check types report SKIP passed = NA instead PASS; summaries report n_pass, n_fail, n_skip. uniqueness family (aliases assert_unique, n_duplicates) executes key-within-group duplicate check. merge_liss_module(strict = TRUE) aborts phase 7 failed severity-error check, outputs written; merge_liss_modules forwards argument (harness A7/A8 corresponding regression tests). C3, audit trace empty rules. rule resolves zero targets writes NO_TARGETS log entry, exactly cr/cp/cs class mis-keyed rules caught (harness A3). C7, two-factor authentication. 2FA step selects verification form “code” field, returns NULL failure, liss_login longer caches failed session authenticated.

title: Cross-Sectional Analysis with a Single Wave
text: cross-sectional design treats one wave population snapshot. appropriate need point--time estimate (prevalence, association) require within-person change. Common examples include Master’s thesis using recent Health wave, methods paper demonstrating new estimator single data release. Even single-wave analysis, lissr adds value: automates authentication, lets pick exactly files need, gives access merge engine’s sentinel-code cleaning start analysis-ready data instead raw SPSS.

what: Step 1 — download one wave of one module
title: Cross-Sectional Analysis with a Single Wave
code: library(lissr)  # authenticate (once per session) liss_login()  # build the file inventory bp <- liss_blueprint()  # filter to the latest Health wave, SPSS format latest_health <- bp |>   dplyr::filter(     module == "Health",     type   == "spss"   ) |>   dplyr::filter(wave == max(wave))  latest_health #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   module module_id  wave wave_id type  name       file              path #>   <chr>      <int> <int>   <int> <chr> <chr>      <chr>             <chr> #> 1 Health        18    18    1102 spss  ch25r 1.0p ch25r_1_0p_EN.sav /down…  # download just that one file liss_download(latest_health, .dir = "data/ch")

what: Step 2 — read and clean the data
title: Cross-Sectional Analysis with a Single Wave
text: can read file directly haven, can leverage merge engine apply recipe’s sentinel-code recoding even single wave. recipe knows numeric codes “don’t know” vs “prefer say” vs genuine missing — cleaning otherwise hand codebook open. merge engine’s output cleaner column names (ch24q prefix), sentinel values already recoded NA, wave_id / wave_year column appended.
code: library(haven) library(dplyr)  # option A: read raw and clean yourself raw <- haven::read_sav("data/ch/ch25r_1_0p_EN.sav") dim(raw) #> [1] 4892  271  # option B: use the merge engine for a single wave # (this applies prefix stripping, sentinel recoding, and labelled policy) recipe <- liss_recipe("ch")  # temporarily trim the recipe to just the wave you need recipe$wave_index <- purrr::keep(   recipe$wave_index,   ~ .x$id == "ch25r" )  result <- merge_liss_module(recipe, data_dir = "data/ch", output_dir = "output") health <- result$data dim(health) #> [1] 4892  265

title: Cross-Sectional Analysis with a Single Wave
text: cross-sectional analyses need demographics: age, sex, education, income. live Background Variables file (avars, separate monthly release), survey module . Critical rule: join nomem_encr . Never use nohouse_encr — household assignments change time. Match fieldwork month, calendar year.
code: # download the background variables file for the same fieldwork period # (check fieldwork_ym in the merged output for the exact month) bg_files <- bp |>   dplyr::filter(     module == "Background Variables",     type   == "spss",     wave   == 202511  # YYYYMM matching the fieldwork period   )  liss_download(bg_files, .dir = "data/avars")  # each avars file also carries this period as its `wave` column avars <- haven::read_sav("data/avars/avars_202511_EN_1_0p.sav") |>   haven::zap_labels() |>   dplyr::select(     nomem_encr,     age       = leeftijd,     sex       = geslacht,     edu_level = oplcat,     hh_income = nettohh_f,     urban     = sted   )  analysis_df <- health |>   dplyr::left_join(avars, by = "nomem_encr")  nrow(analysis_df) #> [1] 4892

title: Cross-Sectional Analysis with a Single Wave

what: Scenario B — write a recipe from scratch
title: Custom Merge Recipes
text: non-core LISS study (e.g. special-purpose module COVID attitudes), write recipe scratch.
code: my_recipe <- list(   meta = list(     module        = "covid",     module_label  = "COVID Attitudes Special Study",     recipe_version = "1.0.0",     created       = format(Sys.Date()),     source_spec   = "covid_codebook_EN.pdf",     covered_waves = list("covid20a", "covid20b", "covid21c"),     schema_version = "1.0.0"   ),    global = list(     id_variable             = "nomem_encr",     wave_variable           = "wave_id",     year_variable           = "wave_year",     labelled_policy         = "to_numeric",     missing_variable_policy = "warn_and_create_na",     strip_label_whitespace  = TRUE   ),    wave_index = list(     list(id = "covid20a", year = 2020, file_pattern = "covid20a_*"),     list(id = "covid20b", year = 2020, file_pattern = "covid20b_*"),     list(id = "covid21c", year = 2021, file_pattern = "covid21c_*")   ),    variable_rules = list(     list(       rule_id     = "V01_strip_prefix",       action      = "strip_prefix",       description = "remove wave prefix from all columns"     )   ),    harmonization_rules = list(     list(       rule_id     = "H01_sentinel_recode",       action      = "recode_to_na",       description = "recode -9 (DK) and -8 (PNTS) to NA",       scope       = "all_numeric",       codes       = list(-9, -8)     )   ),    boundary_rules = list(),   drop_retain_rules = list(),   derived_variables = list(),   validation_checks = list(),    logging = list(     log_file        = "covid_merge_log.jsonl",     report_file     = "covid_merge_report.txt",     summary_artifact = list(enabled = TRUE)   ) )  # validate before first use validate_recipe(my_recipe, "covid_recipe.yml")  # save to disk yaml::write_yaml(my_recipe, "covid_merge_recipe.yml")

what: Scenario C — keep sentinel codes as distinct values
title: Custom Merge Recipes
text: analyses treat “don’t know” “prefer say” informative categories (e.g. survey methodology research studying item non-response). case, skip harmonization rules recode sentinels NA.
code: recipe <- liss_recipe("ch")  # remove all recode_to_na rules recipe$harmonization_rules <- purrr::discard(   recipe$harmonization_rules,   ~ .x$action == "recode_to_na" )  # optionally add a rule to rename sentinels instead of dropping them recipe$harmonization_rules <- append(   recipe$harmonization_rules,   list(list(     rule_id     = "CUSTOM_keep_dk",     action      = "value_recode",     description = "recode -9 to 97 (DK) and -8 to 98 (PNTS) for explicit modelling",     suffixes    = list("001"),     mapping     = list("-9" = 97, "-8" = 98),     waves       = "all"   )) )  validate_recipe(recipe, "ch_keep_sentinels") result <- merge_liss_module(recipe, data_dir = "data/ch", output_dir = "output/sentinels")

what: Scenario D — add comparability contracts to your custom rules
title: Custom Merge Recipes
text: custom recipe introduces boundary future users know , annotate comparability contract:
code: recipe <- liss_recipe("cs")  recipe$boundary_rules <- append(   recipe$boundary_rules,   list(list(     rule_id     = "CUSTOM_B01_sport_freq_redesign",     action      = "add_period_flag",     description = "sport frequency question redesigned in cs20m",     flag_column = "sport_freq_era",     waves_early = list("cs08a", "cs09b", "cs10c", "cs11d", "cs12e",                        "cs13f", "cs14g", "cs15h", "cs16i", "cs17j",                        "cs18k", "cs19l"),     waves_late  = list("cs20m", "cs21n", "cs22o", "cs23p", "cs24q",                        "cs25r"),     early_label = "open_numeric",     late_label  = "categorical",     comparability = list(       status    = "non_comparable",       method    = "no_pool",       rationale = paste(         "pre-cs20m used open numeric entry conditional on participation;",         "post-cs20m uses categorical scale asked unconditionally.",         "do not pool without explicit period interaction."       )     )   )) )  validate_recipe(recipe, "cs_with_custom_boundary")

title: Income Cleaning
text: Self-reported household income LISS panel contains familiar family artefacts: misplaced decimal points, monthly amounts entered annual field, personal income entered household field, placeholder values, sign errors, residual SPSS missing codes. Left place, single misplaced zero moves household across every income bracket analysis uses. liss_clean_income() turns cleaning procedures used live ad hoc analysis scripts rule-driven, fully audited stage lissr pipeline. Every decision rule declared YAML ruleset, every applied decision lands ledger evidence plain-language justification, original values stay returned data, generated report lets reader reconstruct contest modification.

title: Income Cleaning
text: default ruleset ships package single source truth cleaner may : Rules grouped four sections. Preparation rules (P01 P06) resolve columns, attach background demographics, expand bracket codes, rectify sign errors, sweep residual SPSS missing codes. Detection rules (D01 D11) identify implausible cells, unrecoverable placeholder values household-level scale errors robust statistical outliers dataset-level extremes. Correction rules (C01 C06) generate candidate replacement values. finalization rule (F01) handles anything still outside plausible range according disposition: void (default) sets values NA, winsorise clamps -cap values cap, flag ledgers . rule carries id, description, rationale, optional literature references, parameters, enabled switch.
code: library(lissr)  rs <- liss_cleaning_ruleset() print(rs)  system.file("cleaning", "income_cleaning_rules.yml", package = "lissr")

what: Quick start: dry run first, then correct
title: Income Cleaning
text: cleaner operates merged module output. dry run "flag" mode performs full procedure without touching single value; simulated result arrives nethh_proposed every -decision ledger: proposals look right, run default "correct" mode write audit artifacts: third mode, "na_only", voids detected cells without imputing anything, analyses prefer missingness model-based corrections.
code: result <- merge_liss_module(liss_recipe("ci"), data_dir = "data/ci",                             output_dir = "output/ci")  dry <- liss_clean_income(result, mode = "flag")  head(dry$decisions) summary(dry) cleaned <- liss_clean_income(result, output_dir = "output/ci")  cleaned$data$nethh          # cleaned values cleaned$data$nethh_observed # untouched input, always preserved table(cleaned$data$nethh_clean_status, useNA = "ifany")

title: Income Cleaning
text: Every modified cell least one ledger row. scale-error correction looks like : candidates column lists every admissible candidate selection saw, reader can verify applied value closest anchor, valid_min/valid_max columns show constraint window (category bounds reported, global limits otherwise).
code: d <- cleaned$decisions d[d$rule_id == "D06", c("person_id", "wave", "observed", "corrected",                         "candidate_source", "evidence")]  # the justification column carries the full sentence, for example: # "detected by scale_error; replaced with the household_median candidate #  25800 (closest of 4 admissible candidate(s) to the household_median #  25800; constrained to [8000, 150000])"

title: Income Cleaning
text: liss_cleaning_report() renders three artifacts: markdown report whose methodology section generated ruleset (every rule description, rationale, parameters, references), complete decision ledger CSV, engine-shaped JSONL audit log: methodology generated ruleset actually ran, report can never drift code.
code: liss_cleaning_report(cleaned, output_dir = "output/ci")

title: Income Cleaning
text: Every rule can switched re-parameterised per run, overrides recorded report: lasting policy, copy packaged YAML, edit , pass path; validate_cleaning_ruleset() enforces schema:
code: # a stricter volatility requirement for scale-error detection, # no extreme-z net, and a higher plausibility cap cleaned2 <- liss_clean_income(   result,   income_cap = 175000,   disable = c("D10"),   params = list(D06 = list(volatility_min = 0.7)) ) cleaned3 <- liss_clean_income(result, ruleset = "my_income_rules.yml")

code: background <- haven::read_sav("data/avars_201801_EN_1.0p.sav") cleaned <- liss_clean_income(result, background = background)

what: Why the merge engine matters for longitudinal work
title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: LISS panel surveys individuals annually. Exploiting panel structure — within-person change, fixed effects, event studies — makes LISS valuable beyond repeated cross-section. also introduces complications cross-sectional workflow never encounters: Prefix stripping: wave ch07a stores self-rated health ch07a001, ch24q stores ch24q001. merge engine strips wave prefix become s001, making bind_rows() work. Sentinel-code regime changes: early waves use positive sentinels (999 = “don’t know”), later waves switch negative codes (-9). recipe’s harmonization rules recode regimes NA uniformly. Instrument redesigns: items change wording response scale waves. recipe’s boundary rules flag breaks era indicators comparability contracts — essential information deciding whether pool split. Variable renumbering: questionnaire items sometimes get new suffix numbers. recipe’s crosswalk rules map old suffixes new ones get single harmonised column. encoded YAML recipe applied automatically.

what: Step 1 — download all waves of a module
title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
code: library(lissr) library(dplyr)  liss_login() bp <- liss_blueprint()  # all SPSS files for the Health module health_files <- bp |>   filter(module == "Health", type == "spss")  liss_download(health_files, .dir = "data/ch")

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
code: recipe <- liss_recipe("ch") result <- merge_liss_module(recipe, data_dir = "data/ch", output_dir = "output")  panel <- result$data  # the stacked panel has one row per person-wave panel |> count(wave_id) |> print(n = 20) #> # A tibble: 18 × 2 #>    wave_id     n #>    <chr>   <int> #>  1 ch07a    6871 #>  2 ch08b    6386 #>  3 ch09c    6222 #>  ...  (18 waves, ch07a through ch25r; counts are illustrative)

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
code: # how many waves did each respondent participate in? participation <- panel |>   group_by(nomem_encr) |>   summarise(n_waves = n_distinct(wave_id), .groups = "drop")  # distribution of participation (illustrative counts) table(participation$n_waves) #>    1    2    3   ...   16   17   18 #> 1842  801  602   ...  482  723 2103  # balanced sub-panel: respondents present in all 18 waves balanced_ids <- participation |>   filter(n_waves == max(n_waves)) |>   pull(nomem_encr)  length(balanced_ids) #> [1] 2103

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: common concern panel studies whether attrition non-random. sicker people drop , estimated health trend biased upward. baseline health significantly different groups, consider inverse-probability weighting Heckman selection models.
code: # tag respondents who appear in wave 1 but not in the final wave first_wave <- "ch07a" last_wave  <- "ch25r"  baseline <- panel |>   filter(wave_id == first_wave) |>   mutate(     survived = nomem_encr %in%       (panel |> filter(wave_id == last_wave) |> pull(nomem_encr))   )  # compare baseline self-rated health between survivors and attriters # (age is not a survey-module column; attach it from the Background # Variables file if you need it in this comparison) baseline |>   group_by(survived) |>   summarise(     n         = n(),     mean_srh  = mean(s001, na.rm = TRUE),     .groups   = "drop"   ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 3 #>   survived     n mean_srh #>   <lgl>    <int>    <dbl> #> 1 FALSE     4480     3.08 #> 2 TRUE      2103     3.25

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: Person fixed effects absorb time-invariant confounders (genetics, childhood environment, stable personality). workhorse model panel data.
code: library(fixest)  # self-rated health (s001) regressed on a time-varying predictor with # person and year fixed effects. pick the predictor from your module's # codebook and use its harmonised suffix; `x_var` is a placeholder panel <- panel |> mutate(x_var = s004)  # replace s004 with your item  fe_model <- fixest::feols(   s001 ~ x_var | nomem_encr + wave_year,   data = panel )  summary(fe_model)

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: Health recipe declares boundary rules instrument changed: example, e-cigarette dental-hygienist items (suffixes 265-267) exist ch15h onward, item 020 reworded ch24q. rule materialises flag column; respect . merge report (output/ch_merge_report.txt) lists every comparability contract recommended pooling strategy. Check deciding pool across eras.
code: # inspect boundary flags created by the merge engine flag_cols <- grep("_flag$|_period$|_era$|_present$", names(panel),                   value = TRUE) flag_cols #> [1] "cancer_definition_era"        "alzheimer_definition_era" #> [3] "ch_hca_items_present"         "ch_ecig_dental_items_present" #> [5] "ch_years_not_working_present" "ch_covid_n2o_items_present" #> [7] "ch_menopause_module_present"  "s020_wording_period"  # if your analysis touches the e-cigarette items, restrict to waves # where the block was fielded — or include the flag as a control panel |>   filter(ch_ecig_dental_items_present) |>   count(wave_year)

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: Panel data combined dated treatment allows event-study designs. example, suppose policy change affected subset respondents 2016. can estimate dynamic treatment effects:
code: # define treatment: respondents exposed to the policy, identified from # a baseline covariate you attach (for example province of residence # from the Background Variables file). `treated_ids` is your design's # treatment group treated_ids <- c( )  # fill from your design  panel <- panel |>   mutate(     treated  = as.integer(nomem_encr %in% treated_ids),     post     = as.integer(wave_year >= 2016),     rel_year = wave_year - 2016   )  # event-study with staggered treatment es_model <- fixest::feols(   s001 ~ i(rel_year, treated, ref = -1) | nomem_encr + wave_year,   data = panel )  fixest::iplot(es_model, main = "Event study: self-rated health")

what: Step 6 — growth curves and multilevel models
title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: prefer random-effects framework (e.g. prediction -person variation interest), stacked panel feeds directly lme4:
code: library(lme4)  # linear growth curve: health trajectories over time panel <- panel |>   mutate(year_centered = wave_year - 2007)  growth <- lme4::lmer(   s001 ~ year_centered + (1 + year_centered | nomem_encr),   data = panel )  summary(growth)

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: Sometimes need window waves — instance, years around policy change. Rather merging 18 waves, trim recipe merging: faster produces smaller output file, note boundary rules spanning excluded waves fire.
code: recipe <- liss_recipe("ch")  # keep only 2015-2018 (ch skipped 2014; there is no ch14 wave) target_waves <- c("ch15h", "ch16i", "ch17j", "ch18k") recipe$wave_index <- purrr::keep(   recipe$wave_index,   ~ .x$id %in% target_waves )  result <- merge_liss_module(recipe, data_dir = "data/ch", output_dir = "output")

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: Every merge produces JSONL log (ch_merge_log.jsonl) recording rule application timestamps, row counts, NA deltas. gives transparent audit trail Supplementary Materials section paper — can show exactly transformations applied raw data.
code: log <- jsonlite::stream_in(file("output/ch_merge_log.jsonl"), verbose = FALSE)  # how many values were recoded to NA? log |>   filter(action == "recode_to_na") |>   summarise(total_recoded = sum(values_changed, na.rm = TRUE))

title: Longitudinal Panel Analysis
text: Used merge_liss_module() equivalent stack waves harmonised column names. Checked merge report comparability contracts boundary flags pooling across eras. Reported attrition rates tested selective dropout. Specified estimator (FE, RE, FD) justified research question. using person fixed effects, verified outcome sufficient within-person variation across waves. Attached time-varying demographics correct fieldwork-month background variables files. Cited LISS wave identifiers recipe version methods section.

title: Merging LISS Panel Data
text: lissr includes recipe-driven merge engine processes YAML specifications conforming Canonical Schema v1.1.0 (strictly additive extension v1.0.0; recipes may declare either). recipe encodes merge-relevant decisions module: file patterns, variable harmonization, boundary handling, comparability contracts, validation checks. merged output written SPSS .sav format preserve variable labels, value labels, user-defined missing values.

title: Merging LISS Panel Data
text: produces four files: ch_merged.sav — merged data (SPSS format, preserving labels) ch_merge_log.jsonl — audit-grade structured log ch_merge_summary.json — per-run summary (enabled recipe) ch_merge_report.txt — human-readable report returned object additionally carries provenance (package, recipe, schema versions, per-input md5 hashes, release-selection decisions) valid_for_analysis verdict, reproduced report.
code: library(lissr)  recipe <- liss_recipe("ch") result <- merge_liss_module(   recipe,   data_dir   = "liss/ch",   output_dir = "./output" )

title: Merging LISS Panel Data

title: Merging LISS Panel Data
text: Background Variables file (avars) monthly snapshot used linkage backbone modules. separate release, one ten core modules. merging background variables survey data: Use nomem_encr join key (never nohouse_encr). Match fieldwork month survey, calendar year. merged output includes fieldwork_ym column (YYYYMM integer) derived automatically LISS _m suffix. avars file carries fieldwork period wave column (YYYYMM), stacking monthly files needs filename parsing:
code: # example: merge Health survey with background variables survey <- haven::read_sav("output/ch_merged.sav")  # read avars files; the period is the `wave` column in each file bg_files <- list.files("data/avars/", pattern = "\\.sav$", full.names = TRUE) bg_data  <- purrr::map_dfr(bg_files, function(f) {   haven::read_sav(f) |> dplyr::mutate(fieldwork_ym = as.integer(wave)) })  merged <- dplyr::left_join(   survey, bg_data,   by = c("nomem_encr", "fieldwork_ym") )

what: When you need data from multiple modules
title: Multi-Module Linkage
text: Many research questions span LISS domains. instance: Health income: job loss predict decline self-rated health? needs Health (CH) Economic Integration (CI) modules. Personality political participation: conscientiousness openness predict voter turnout? needs Personality (CP) Politics Values (CV). Housing, family structure, well-: moving home-ownership improve life satisfaction? needs Housing (CD), Family (CF), well-item Health (CH). LISS panel’s key advantage modules share respondent identifier (nomem_encr) can linked person-wave level.

title: Multi-Module Linkage
text: module merged independently using recipe (different sentinel codes, different boundary rules, different variable numbering), cleaned outputs joined nomem_encr + wave_year.
code: Background Variables (avars, monthly) ── demographics, income, education           │           ├── Health (ch) ──── self-rated health, smoking           │           ├── Income (ci) ──── employment status, labour income           │           └── Politics (cv) ── voting, political trust

title: Multi-Module Linkage
text: Alternatively, use batch interface:
code: library(lissr) library(dplyr) library(purrr)  liss_login()  # download all waves for three modules. map each module code to a # distinctive fragment of its archive display name; matching by # fragment is robust to small display-name changes, and the code (not # the display name) names the directory the recipes expect bp <- liss_blueprint() mods <- c(ch = "Health", ci = "Income", cv = "Politics") for (code in names(mods)) {   files <- bp |>     filter(grepl(mods[[code]], module, ignore.case = TRUE), type == "spss")   liss_download(files, .dir = file.path("data", code)) }  # merge each module with its own recipe modules <- c("ch", "ci", "cv") results <- purrr::map(modules, function(mod) {   recipe <- liss_recipe(mod)   merge_liss_module(     recipe,     data_dir   = file.path("data", mod),     output_dir = file.path("output", mod)   ) }) |> purrr::set_names(modules) recipe_paths <- purrr::map_chr(modules, ~ {   system.file("recipes", paste0(.x, "_merge_recipe.yml"), package = "lissr") })  results <- merge_liss_modules(recipe_paths, data_dir = "data", output_dir = "output")

title: Multi-Module Linkage

code: # full join preserves all person-years from any module linked <- health |>   full_join(income,   by = c("nomem_encr", "wave_year")) |>   full_join(politics, by = c("nomem_encr", "wave_year"))  dim(linked) #> [1] 125438      7  # restrict to the common years for a balanced design linked_balanced <- linked |>   filter(wave_year %in% common_years)

title: Multi-Module Linkage
text: Background variables supply time-varying demographics (age, education level, household income) part survey module.
code: # read all monthly background variable files bg_files <- list.files("data/avars", pattern = "\\.sav$", full.names = TRUE) bg <- purrr::map_dfr(bg_files, \(f) {   haven::read_sav(f) |>     haven::zap_labels() |>     select(nomem_encr,            fieldwork_ym = wave,            age       = leeftijd,            sex       = geslacht,            edu       = oplcat,            hh_income = nettohh_f,            urban     = sted) })  # create a wave_year column to join on bg <- bg |>   mutate(wave_year = as.integer(substr(fieldwork_ym, 1, 4))) |>   # keep one snapshot per person-year (e.g. November of each year)   group_by(nomem_encr, wave_year) |>   slice_max(fieldwork_ym, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) |>   ungroup()  linked <- linked |>   left_join(bg, by = c("nomem_encr", "wave_year"))

text: need variables every module, batch merge handles :
code: all_modules <- c("ch", "cv", "cd", "cf", "cw", "cp", "cs", "ci", "ca", "cr") recipe_paths <- purrr::map_chr(all_modules, ~ {   system.file("recipes", paste0(.x, "_merge_recipe.yml"), package = "lissr") })  all_results <- merge_liss_modules(   recipe_paths, data_dir = "data", output_dir = "output" )  # join all modules progressively linked_all <- all_results[[1]]$data |>   select(nomem_encr, wave_year, srh = s001)  for (mod in names(all_results)[-1]) {   vars_to_keep <- c("nomem_encr", "wave_year",                      # pick a few variables from each                      head(setdiff(names(all_results[[mod]]$data),                                   c("nomem_encr", "wave_id", "wave_year")), 5))   linked_all <- linked_all |>     full_join(       all_results[[mod]]$data |> select(all_of(vars_to_keep)),       by = c("nomem_encr", "wave_year")     ) }  dim(linked_all)

what: The reproducibility problem in survey data
title: Reproducible Research Pipelines
text: Published analyses longitudinal survey data notoriously hard reproduce. raw files contain labelled-value quirks, sentinel codes, variable renumbering across waves, undocumented instrument changes. Every research team writes cleaning scripts, small differences sentinel codes handled can change substantive conclusions. lissr’s recipe system addresses encoding cleaning decisions single, declarative YAML file : version-controlled alongside analysis code, validated formal schema data touched, produces structured audit log every transformation applied. vignette shows build full reproducible pipeline around tools.

title: Reproducible Research Pipelines
text: recommended layout LISS-based research project: data/ output/ gitignored — contain individual-level data shared. recipes, analysis scripts, golden references, test files committed form replication package.
code: my-project/ ├── data/                    # raw LISS downloads (gitignored) │   ├── ch/ │   └── avars/ ├── recipes/                 # YAML merge recipes (version-controlled) │   └── ch_custom.yml ├── output/                  # merged outputs (gitignored) │   ├── ch_merged.sav │   ├── ch_merge_log.jsonl │   ├── ch_merge_report.txt │   └── ch_merge_summary.json ├── golden/                  # golden reference files for regression tests │   └── ch_golden.json ├── R/ │   ├── 01_download.R │   ├── 02_merge.R │   ├── 03_analysis.R │   └── 04_figures.R ├── tests/ │   └── test_merge_regression.R └── README.md

code: # run after the first successful merge merged <- haven::read_sav("output/ch_merged.sav")  golden <- list(   row_count          = nrow(merged),   col_count          = ncol(merged),   wave_count         = length(unique(merged$wave_id)),   unique_respondents = length(unique(merged$nomem_encr)),   column_names       = sort(names(merged)),   wave_ids           = sort(unique(merged$wave_id)),   wave_row_counts    = as.list(table(merged$wave_id)),   na_rates           = lapply(merged, \(x) round(mean(is.na(x)), 4)),   column_types       = vapply(merged, \(x) class(x)[1], character(1)),   # from the merge result: pin the recipe version and the quality verdict   recipe_version     = result$provenance$recipe_version,   valid_for_analysis = result$valid_for_analysis )  jsonlite::write_json(golden, "golden/ch_golden.json",                      pretty = TRUE, auto_unbox = TRUE)

title: Reproducible Research Pipelines
code: # tests/test_merge_regression.R library(testthat) library(jsonlite)  test_that("CH merge output matches golden reference", {   merged <- haven::read_sav("output/ch_merged.sav")   golden <- jsonlite::read_json("golden/ch_golden.json")    expect_equal(nrow(merged), golden$row_count)   expect_equal(ncol(merged), golden$col_count)   expect_equal(length(unique(merged$wave_id)), golden$wave_count)   expect_equal(sort(names(merged)), golden$column_names)    # check per-wave row counts   actual_wave_counts <- as.list(table(merged$wave_id))   for (w in names(golden$wave_row_counts)) {     expect_equal(       actual_wave_counts[[w]],       golden$wave_row_counts[[w]],       label = paste("wave", w, "row count")     )   }    # check NA rate drift (warn at 5 percentage points)   for (col in names(golden$na_rates)) {     if (col %in% names(merged)) {       actual_na <- round(mean(is.na(merged[[col]])), 4)       expected_na <- golden$na_rates[[col]]       expect_lt(         abs(actual_na - expected_na), 0.05,         label = paste("NA rate drift in", col)       )     }   } })

text: Credentials stored operating system’s keyring (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Store, Linux Secret Service); keyring package dependency installs lissr.
code: library(lissr)  # 1. store credentials (once, prompts for password) liss_store_credentials("1234")  # 2. log in (credentials retrieved from keyring + 2FA prompt) liss_login()  # 3. explore liss_modules() liss_wave_matrix()  # 4. interactively select modules, waves, file types selection <- liss_select()  # 5. download liss_download(selection)  # 6. merge a module using the built-in recipe recipe <- liss_recipe("ch") result <- merge_liss_module(recipe, data_dir = "liss", output_dir = "./output")  # 7. batch merge all core modules modules <- c("ch", "cv", "cd", "cf", "cw", "cp", "cs", "ci", "ca", "cr") results <- merge_liss_modules(   purrr::map_chr(modules, ~ system.file("recipes", paste0(.x, "_merge_recipe.yml"),                                          package = "lissr")),   data_dir = "liss",   output_dir = "./output" )

Custom Merge Recipes: writing adapting YAML recipe canonical schema. Income Cleaning: rule-driven detection constrained correction implausible household-income values, full audit ledger. Reproducible Research Pipelines: structuring workflow reproducible pipeline. Canonical Recipe Schema: authoritative YAML structure every recipe conforms , rendered packaged schema document.

title: View, Download, and Merge LISS Panel Data
text: merge engine processes YAML recipes conforming CANONICAL_SCHEMA.md, schema version 1.1.0 (strictly additive extension 1.0.0; recipes may declare either, recipe additionally carries recipe_version). recipe encodes every merge-relevant decision module: wave file patterns, variable harmonization rules, boundary handling, comparability contracts, validation checks. Every merged output carries provenance (package, recipe, schema versions, per-input md5 hashes, release-selection decisions) explicit valid_for_analysis verdict, returned object text report. audit_liss_recipes() produces corpus-level conformance report bundled recipes, suitable CI gating. Built-recipes included ten core LISS modules: ch (Health), cv (Politics Values), cd (Housing), cf (Family Household), cw (Work Schooling), cp (Personality), cs (Culture Sports), ci (Economic Integration: Income), ca (Assets), cr (Religion Ethnicity). Background Variables file (avars) separate monthly release, one modules.

what: C1. value_recode applies mappings sequentially and can chain
title: lissr: Improvement Review
text: exec_harmonization_rule(), value_recode na_recode/recode branches compute mask live, already-mutated column: mapping target equals later mapping source, values recoded twice. cr religion crosswalks exactly shape. cr HR10 maps {1:1, 2:2, 3:4, 4:5, 5:3, 6:3, 7:6, 8:8, 9:9, 10:11, 11:10, 12:7, 13:12, 14:13}. Traced sequential loop: original 3 becomes 4, caught 4: 5 step becomes 5, caught 5: 3 ends 3. intended 3 4 mapping never survives. HR11 HR12 overlap structure (11 13 colliding targets ). Notably, engine author already solved correctly elsewhere: crosswalk_rename post_recode block explicitly “matches snapshot multi-entry maps chain”, crosswalk action goes crosswalk_map(), also reads frozen source. fix give value_recode recode snapshot semantics: Add unit test intentionally chaining map, example {1: 2, 2: 3} c(1, 2), asserting c(2, 3) rather c(3, 3).
code: for (from_val in names(mapping)) {   mask <- !is.na(df[[col]]) & df[[col]] == as.numeric(from_val)   df[[col]][mask] <- as.numeric(to_val) } # snapshot-based recode: all masks computed against the original vector orig <- df[[col]] changed <- 0L for (from_val in names(mapping)) {   to_val <- mapping[[from_val]]   mask <- !is.na(orig) & orig == as.numeric(from_val)   n <- sum(mask)   if (n > 0) {     df[[col]][mask] <- if (is.null(to_val) || identical(to_val, ".na")) NA                        else as.numeric(to_val)     changed <- changed + n   } }

what: C2. The cr religion harmonization never runs at all: suffix vs suffixes
title: lissr: Improvement Review
text: cr HR10, HR11, HR12 (HR20, HR21) use singular key suffix:. engine reads rule$suffixes %||% rule$stems %||% rule$variables; singular suffix RECOGNIZED_RULE_KEYS, target list resolves list() loop body executes zero times. Two consequences: three-era religion crosswalk, described CANONICAL_SCHEMA.md working feature (“Religion harmonization (cr) maps three coding eras onto one coarse 1-13 scheme”), entirely inert. Merged cr output carries three incompatible coding schemes column recode era-safe pooling. log entry emitted rules, JSONL audit trail contains trace rule skipped. TODO.md documents cr BR20/BR21/BR30 pending HR10-HR12, appears unknown silent failure rather known limitation. Fix two layers: rename suffix: suffixes: cr recipe (HR20/HR21), land C1 first, key fixed chaining bug corrupt crosswalk repaired. Also consider re-expressing crosswalk rules output_variable, sidesteps chaining construction gains unmapped-code coverage check free.

what: C3. Rules that emit no log entry when their target list is empty break auditability
title: lissr: Improvement Review
text: Generalizing C2: several executor branches (value_recode, set_label, fix_label, others) loop resolved targets log inside loop. rule whose scoping key mis-named, whose variables absent, produces neither transformation log line. engine marketed “audit-grade”, every rule leave least one entry per wave scheduled , including explicit NO_TARGETS marker: cheap post-merge invariant also worth adding: every rule_id recipe (minus note_only) must appear least log, otherwise warn.

what: C8. Multi-file wave matches are silently stacked; the cd10c case works only by accident
title: lissr: Improvement Review
text: discover_wave_files() keeps every path pattern matches, pipeline dplyr::bind_rows(dfs) one. actual data directory (per CSV) contains cd10c_EN_1.0p.sav cd10c_EN_1.1p.sav. recipe’s file_pattern: cd10c_EN_1_0p.csv matches neither real file (see H1), prefix fallback ^cd10c[_.] matches , stacked. cd specifically, recipe asserts intended: aux_files note says “stack ; zero respondent overlap (anomaly 9)”, meaning 1.1p supplemental release disjoint respondents. three things make current behavior fragile: mechanism supposed express intent, stack_aux_files, pending_spec -op; stacking happens fallback glob accidentally -matches. Anyone “fixes” file pattern silently drop 1.1p respondents; anyone whose archive copy 1.1p superseding full re-release (common LISS pattern version bumps) get every wave-10 respondent duplicated. safety checks written guard (CHK06, CHK07) never execute (C6). every module, stray second match (old version left folder, .dta alongside .sav, partial download) duplicates respondents error, duplicates propagate multiplicatively merge_liss_panel()’s sequential joins. Recommended design: make multi-file behavior explicit defensive. Pair first-class aux_files contract: stacking, assert zero nomem_encr overlap primary aux (erroring otherwise), turns cd10c note executed guarantee. add unconditional per-wave duplicate-id check phase 1 regardless recipe content; duplicated respondent-wave rows never valid modules.
code: # inside discover_wave_files(), after matching if (length(found) > 1) {   aux <- w$aux_files %||% character(0)   is_aux <- basename(found) %in% aux |     tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(found)) %in% tools::file_path_sans_ext(aux)   primary <- found[!is_aux]   if (length(primary) > 1) {     # prefer the highest release version when several primaries match     ver <- stringr::str_match(basename(primary), "_(\\d+(?:[._]\\d+)?)p?")[ , 2] %>%       gsub("_", ".", .) %>% as.numeric()     if (all(!is.na(ver))) {       keep <- primary[which.max(ver)]       cli::cli_warn("wave {w$id}: {length(primary)} candidate files; using {basename(keep)}")       primary <- keep     } else {       cli::cli_abort("wave {w$id}: multiple files match and versions cannot be ranked; disambiguate on disk or in the recipe")     }   }   found <- c(primary, found[is_aux]) }

code: parse_waves <- function(x) {   x <- gsub("\\s", "", x)   if (!grepl("^[0-9]+([:,-][0-9]+)*$", x)) return(NULL)   parts <- strsplit(x, ",", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]   out <- lapply(parts, function(p) {     if (grepl("[:-]", p)) {       bounds <- as.integer(strsplit(p, "[:-]")[[1]])       seq.int(bounds[1], bounds[2])     } else as.integer(p)   })   sort(unique(unlist(out))) }

title: lissr: Empirical Verification Report
text: Companion lissr-review.md. Every claim computed uploaded bundle (lissr_verification_bundle.zip, 35 data files). Integrity: 35 md5 checksums match bundle’s MANIFEST.tsv original files_liss-siardv_20260701_214334.csv inventory, analyzed bytes exactly archive copies. Methodological note finding (corrected). .sav files declare DK/refusal codes SPSS user-defined missing values. default pyreadstat read masks NA, first-pass scan defaults found almost sentinels; re-reading user_missing = TRUE revealed tens thousands . earlier revision note claimed preserving codes “haven::read_sav() (therefore lissr) ”; direct measurement patch development showed wrong. haven’s default user_na = FALSE, also converts declared user-missing codes NA read, 1.0.0 engine used default. Three corrected implications follow. First, shipped 1.0.0 engine recipes’ sentinel recodes largely never saw declared codes, read step already masked ; recodes doubly inert (mis-keyed rules, pre-masked inputs). masking silent, unlogged, collapsed DK-vs-refusal distinction, missed sentinel wave failed declare (undeclared ones exactly values reach outputs). Second, scans report, made user_missing = TRUE, describe data actually contain engine reading haven::read_sav(user_na = TRUE) sees; 1.1.0 engine reads way deliberately, recipes’ recodes receive codes handle auditably. Third, lissr’s to_numeric policy plus haven::write_sav() discarded user-missing declarations entirely; 1.1.0 label round-trip restores provably safe sweeps remainder per wave. Preserving DK-vs-refusal distinction beyond (example via haven::tagged_na()) remains follow-work (review C9).

what: 8. Consolidated deltas to the original review
title: lissr: Empirical Verification Report
text: C8 upgraded design fragility confirmed active corruption: archive, shipped cd configuration duplicates 3,626 wave-10 respondents resurrects redacted open-text location field. Section 1’s recipe patch fixes cd immediately; engine-side version logic remains systemic fix. now unambiguously priority 1 alongside cr pair. C1/C2 quantified: 86 (2.9%) 312 (14.0%) misclassified respondents per wave eras 1 2 key fixed without snapshot fix; zero era 3 luck map ordering. mapping tables fully validated correct, entire defect mechanical cheap fix. C4/C5 quantified bounded: cp08a collateral exactly 3 duration cells (234 intended); cs08a’s inert recode leaves 211 DK cells silent read-time masking instead documented -9 convention; cv08a’s inert HR01 likewise leaves 1,147 DK cells unlogged masking. new cross-cutting recommendation emerges: sentinel scopes exclude paradata durations , better, anchor sentinel code’s value label. See corrected methodological note read-semantics nuance revises earlier “raw codes ship output” phrasing. New finding: user-missing declarations destroyed to_numeric plus write_sav path; fold preservation C9 label round-trip. Recipes’ domain knowledge holds : everywhere checks bite, declarative content (mappings, wave scoping, sentinel regimes, target suffixes) proved correct data codebooks; failures concentrated execution layer (unread keys, sequential recode, unimplemented check types, accidental file matching). encouraging: expensive research work sound, fixes engineering. title: Equivalise household income — liss_equivalise_income
text: Converts household income per-equivalent-adult scale cross-household comparison. default "weighted_sqrt" scale divides (adults + child_weight * children)^elasticity, scale used source analysis pipelines; "oecd_modified" divides 1 + 0.5 * (adults - 1) + 0.3 * children; "sqrt" divides square root household size. title: lissr 1.4.0
text: nine-stage, evidence-driven overhaul merge engine, bundled recipes, income cleaner, download authentication layer, documentation. Every stage landed regression tests green R CMD check; test suite grew 448 roughly 760 assertions. stage--stage record follows highlights. Highlights: Merge engine correctness: fieldwork_ym populated across nine declaring modules (blanked ordering bug), cross-wave type harmonization factor-safe, every rule application leaves audit trace. Recipe conformance, made permanent: per-action payload validation driven vocabulary registry, check-type grammar 99 bundled validation checks classify 68 executable 31 documentary (none silently skipped), exported audit_liss_recipes() corpus report suitable CI gating. End--end assurance: every bundled recipe merges synthetic panel test suite strict assertions; merged outputs carry provenance (versions, input md5 hashes, release decisions) explicit valid_for_analysis verdict; release-version pins overwrite guard protect outputs. Output-changing semantic repairs, verified archive evidence: cf module rebuilt 17-wave scan (exact recode counts reproduced real data), ca’s wave_year standardized fieldwork year asset reference year preserved per respondent, cv’s fieldwork months made executable, cr’s 2019 scale breaks expressed era-scoped output columns. Income cleaning: eight policy defects repaired (anchor dispatch, range-fallback voiding, single-person zero-gross gates, bracket mapping, household-id fallback join diagnostics, scoped enable_only, configurable F01 disposition, equivalisation guards), ruleset 1.1.0, controlled-value validation, adversarial fixture suite. Download authentication hardening: HTTP errors never written data, verified unzip protects sole copies, session expiry aborts batches, downloads stream timeout retries, credentials never echoed, 2FA retryable guarded, login probe cheap RNG-neutral, keyring moved Imports. Documentation rebuilt reality, drift tests re-derive every factual claim code recipes, canonical-schema article rendered packaged schema build time.

title: lissr 1.4.0
text: documentation now describes package preceding stages built, drift-test suite (tests/testthat/test-stage8-docs.R) re-derives every factual claim code bundled recipes two diverge silently . Corrected content: Background Variables file longer called “module (CA)” anywhere (separate monthly release; ca Assets); every “eight core modules” claim now says ten batch examples include ca cr; multi-module download loop longer derives directory names truncating display names (bug wrote Health files data//) matches modules name fragment instead exact display strings; longitudinal vignette’s fabricated columns (s002 age, s038 BMI) undefined treated variable replaced real recipe-declared flag columns (ch_ecig_dental_items_present, s020_wording_period, cancer/alzheimer era flags) explicitly reader-supplied treatment definition; reproducible-pipelines CI chunk installs lissr GitHub instead install_local(".") non-package research project. Settled facts: avars files carry fieldwork period wave column (YYYYMM), merge-workflow example reads directly instead parsing filenames (multi-module vignette already ); wave counts year lists reflect current recipes (ch 18 waves, ch07a-ch25r, skipping 2014; cv skips 2015 reaches cv26r; cw 15); str(recipe) VALID_ACTIONS listings show real current output; documented schema version v1.1.0 everywhere (packaged schema’s version), additive relationship v1.0.0 stated. Additions: new “Canonical Recipe Schema” article renders inst/schema/CANONICAL_SCHEMA.md build time (zero duplication, drift); README gains income-cleaning schema articles vignette list, directory--naming-contract section, keyring-ships--lissr note, provenance/valid_for_analysis audit_liss_recipes() coverage, module labels matching recipes; pkgdown reference index now lists audit_liss_recipes() (absence failed site build) new article. Drift tests: “(CA)” mislabel eight-module claim anywhere; batch lists must contain exactly ten module codes; action vocabulary str() listings custom-recipes must equal installed package’s reality; flag names quoted vignettes must exist recipes; wave-count claims must match recipes’ wave_index lengths; documented schema version must match packaged schema. README assertions run source tree skip R CMD check.

title: lissr 1.4.0
text: network layer longer reports failure success, longer destroys data failure, fails loudly diagnosably archive’s pages drift. /O surface reduced three small shims, every error path exercised offline mocked tests (tests/testthat/test-stage7-network.R). N1, download integrity: HTTP error responses detected status code never written disk data files (404 page previously became foo.sav status “ok”); ZIP archives extracted verification archive deleted extracted files exist (corrupt zip previously triggered deletion copy); first session expiry aborts remaining batch status skipped_batch_aborted instead failing file file. N5, transfer robustness: downloads stream disk session’s cookie handle (whole-file buffering memory), carry per-file .timeout, retry transient failures (curl errors HTTP 5xx, never deterministic 4xx) .retries, can skip files already present via .skip_existing. Partial transfers land temporary name renamed status check. N2, confirmation logic: “download everything?” prompt now defaults , skipped explicit selection tibble passed, non-interactive full-archive request aborts guidance instead silently auto-confirming. N3, credential hygiene: failed-login message longer echoes masked password (first character plus stars); empty unreadable keyring password aborts login re-store hint instead crashing strrep(). N4, form discovery: login page without expected form username/password fields aborts diagnosis naming found, instead subscript---bounds error. N6, content-disposition: header parsing supports quoted, unquoted, RFC 5987 filename*= forms (unquoted form previously yielded whole header file name), every resulting name sanitized (path components stripped, traversal reserved characters rejected). N7, blueprint cache: failed module wave pages tallied reported; partial scrape caches explicit incompleteness warning, empty scrape aborts caches nothing, unreachable module index aborts immediately. N8, two-factor entry: code whitespace-normalized (email copy-paste), rejected code can retried three times without restarting login, kicked-back session aborts guidance, non-interactive sessions abort prompting (previously empty string submitted silently). N9, liss_is_logged_in(): probe now cheap side-effect free. cached blueprint HEADs first known protected file path (body transfer, deterministic choice); without one inspects login page. longer triggers full archive scrape, downloads random data file, mutates RNG state. N11, keyring placement: keyring moves Suggests Imports; already unconditionally required credentialed use, install-time guards pretending otherwise gone (three permanently-skipping guard tests).

title: lissr 1.4.0
text: Eight policy defects liss_clean_income() destroy fabricate values real data repaired, ruleset validator now enforces controlled values, adversarial fixture suite pins every repair (tests/testthat/test-stage6-cleaning.R). C1: selection.anchor now executed, merely reported. engine dispatches candidate-selection statistic (household_median household_mean) ruleset’s selection block, generated report can longer claim method code use; unknown anchors rejected load time. C2: range_midpoint correction fallback (C06) additionally requires anchor lie inside admissible range. -minimum household (e.g. 5000/5200/50000/5100 min_income 8000) previously 10x entry error “corrected” range midpoint 79000, fifteen times truth, ledgered confident correction; cells now voided explicit justification. C3: D04 (personal-income echo) fires households known hold least min_household_size (default 2) members, resolved new variables.household_size mapping (aantalhh); single-person household echo expected truth, roughly 40 percent Dutch households single-person. D03 (contextual floor) requires positive gross personal income instead merely finite one, “household income near zero gross personal 0” longer voids. C4: bracket-code expansion (P04) classifies category column share nonzero finite values 1..max_code (new code_share param, default 0.9) maps per value. One stray sentinel longer silently disables mapping whole column (raw codes misread euro bounds D07 disarmed); stray codes warned treated missing brackets, ambiguous mixtures declined loudly. C5: rows missing household id fall back person-id grouping household stage instead silently receiving cleaning; skipped fallback row counts appear summary, log, report. background join (P01) detects background wave scale (yyyymm, calendar year, annual wavenr) instead assuming yyyymm, reports match rate, warns nothing matches (year-keyed background previously produced -NA join logged success). C6: enable_only scoped per ruleset section: sections containing named rule restricted, enable_only = "D06" isolates one detection rule preparation, correction, finalization machinery keep running (previously detected cell voided correction rules disabled ). C7: F01 hard cap gains disposition: void (default, old behavior), winsorise (-cap values clamp cap; non-positive values still void), flag (ledger , values retained). Genuine top incomes exist, unconditional voiding biases right tail downward; choice now explicit, per-ruleset per-call. C9: equivalisation guards: zero-adult compositions yield NA (OECD-modified divisor fall 1), composition vectors intermediate length error instead silently recycling, documentation states aantalki (children home age) approximates OECD -14 child definition. A8: validate_cleaning_ruleset() enforces controlled values detection-rule disposition/scope/stage, finalization disposition, selection anchors, consensus-detector methods (consensus bounded method count), nested per-method thresholds. Audit consistency: D01’s ledger variable field longer renders literal “NA/…” bounds column unresolved; P03, D01, F01 dispositions emit proper JSONL log entries; per-rule log: false now suppresses rule’s per-rule decision aggregation JSONL trace (decision ledger never suppressed); liss_cleaning_report() requires output_dir instead writing three files working directory default. Tests: three cli-rendered assertions width-fragile (multi-word regexes break cli wraps words) now match across wrap points.

Every behavior exercised regression tests tests/testthat/test-clean-income.R seeded end--end smoke run (inst/scripts/verification/income_cleaning_smoke.R); architecture, rule catalog, mapping legacy analysis scripts live repository’s INCOME_CLEANING_DESIGN.md.

title: lissr 1.2.0
text: New liss_clean_income() detects, evaluates, corrects implausible household-income values declarative YAML ruleset (inst/cleaning/income_cleaning_rules.yml, schema 1.0.0) 24 rules: six preparation rules (P01-P06), eleven detectors (D01-D11), six correction-candidate generators (C01-C06), one finalizer (F01). Rules dispatch action, evaluate ruleset order, every rule carries description, rationale, parameters, literature references generated report reproduces. Full decision transparency. Original values preserved unchanged <target>_observed; every modified cell marked <target>_clean_status final action rule; every decision, applied proposed, lands 18-column typed ledger responsible rule, evidence, admissible candidate set sources, anchor, valid range, plain-language justification. liss_cleaning_report() renders methodology directly ruleset plus decision appendix, writes ledger CSV engine-shaped JSONL audit log. Three modes. correct applies corrections; flag true dry run whose <target>_proposed column identical correct write data remain untouched; na_only voids detected cells without imputing. Re-running already-cleaned data aborts (*_observed column acts double-cleaning guard). Researcher control. income_cap, min_income, disable, enable_only, per-rule params, variables mapping, custom ruleset files honored recorded run metadata report, reviewer can reproduce configured run report alone. Seeded smoke evidence: 400 synthetic households (2,168 rows) 158 planted errors across seven families, recovery 100 percent every family (65/65 decimal shifts, 33/33 extra zeros, 9/9 cap blowouts, 22/22 personal-income echoes, 11/11 tiny junk values, 11/11 sign flips, 7/7 residual sentinels), 0 2,010 clean cells falsely modified, 83 percent scale corrections land within 10 percent true value (median relative error 3.8 percent, simulation’s noise floor), run completes half second. modified-z detector (D10) gained min_relative_deviation gate (default 0.3) smoke run exposed ungated legacy criterion rewrote 12.09 percent clean cells tight households, tiny MAD inflates z-score ordinary variation. gate removes every false positive recall unchanged; tight-household case pinned regression suite. liss_equivalise_income() converts household income per-equivalent-adult scale (weighted_sqrt, matching source pipelines’ stand_inc formula, plus oecd_modified sqrt). New exports: liss_clean_income(), liss_cleaning_ruleset(), validate_cleaning_ruleset(), liss_cleaning_report(), liss_equivalise_income(), print summary methods run results rulesets. new vignette, income-cleaning, walks workflow. new dependencies. Temporal smoothing uses native weighted moving average (equivalent imputeTS::na_ma linear weighting, including window widening), numeric kernels base R.

title: lissr 1.1.0
text: Value recodes now use snapshot semantics. value_recode, recode_to_na, recode masks built column stood rule started, overlapping maps can longer chain. real data old sequential loop misclassified 86 2,992 answered respondents (2.9 percent) cr08a 312 2,222 (14.0 percent) cr14g religion crosswalks made run; snapshot engine reproduces verified target distributions exactly. recode_to_na accepts recode: alias sentinel map honors wave-scoped exclude: blocks (skip cells whose suffix wave match). activates cv module’s HR01 HR04 sentinel rules, previously validated never executed. Superseded-release protection. several primary files match one wave’s file_pattern, engine ranks release versions parsed file names, keeps highest, warns; unrankable candidates abort. Auxiliary files declared via aux_files resolve independently primary pattern must disjoint primary file id variable; shared respondent id aborts. duplicate-id gate runs every wave regardless. Previously pattern matches stacked, duplicated 3,626 cd10c respondents 1.0p superseding 1.1p release disk, resurrected field 1.1p release redacted. read_wave_file reads extension whitelist (.sav, .zsav, .dta, .csv) aborts anything else instead parsing CSV. SPSS files read user_na = TRUE, declared DK/refusal codes reach recipes values instead silently converted NA read. Value labels user-missing declarations round-trip labelled_policy: to_numeric. Metadata stashed per wave read time restored write time provably safe (identical metadata across waves, every observed value accounted ); columns restored pass per-wave residual sweep converts codes wave declared user-missing NA, honoring recipe exclude blocks veto. Outputs regain value labels (75 labelled columns cv verification merge) DK refusal code can leak output substantive value. Rules resolve zero target columns write NO_TARGETS entry JSONL log, mis-keyed mis-scoped rules visible audit trail. Validation checks name unimplemented type report SKIP passed = NA instead PASS; summaries report n_pass, n_fail, n_skip. New uniqueness check family (aliases assert_unique, n_duplicates). New strict argument merge_liss_module() merge_liss_modules(): failed checks severity error abort output written. Default FALSE preserves 1.0.0 behavior. Two-factor authentication returns NULL failure instead half-initialized session, liss_login() can longer cache unauthenticated session logged .

title: lissr 1.0.0
text: First public release. Recipe-driven merge engine. Longitudinal LISS waves merged declarative YAML recipes conform canonical schema (CANONICAL_SCHEMA.md, schema version 1.0.0). recipe captures every merge-relevant decision module: wave file patterns, variable harmonization, boundary handling, comparability contracts, validation checks. Controlled action vocabulary fail-fast validation. Recipes validated merge runs via validate_recipe() (also called load_recipe() merge_liss_module()); unknown actions malformed rules rejected front. Authoring-time check unrecognized rule keys. validate_recipe() emits non-fatal warning listing rule-level key merge engine neither consults sanctions documentation, mis-named keys surfaced load time rather ignored silently. check warning-; every recipe still loads merges unchanged. recognized set documentation allow-list documented CANONICAL_SCHEMA.md. Audit-grade JSONL logging, per-run summary artifact. Ten built-module recipes: Assets (ca), Housing (cd), Family Household (cf), Health (ch), Economic Integration (ci), Personality (cp), Religion Ethnicity (cr), Culture Sports (cs), Politics Values (cv), Work Schooling (cw). Authentication LISS Data Archive two-factor verification; credentials stored via system keyring. Interactive browse, select, download workflow (liss_modules(), liss_wave_matrix(), liss_select(), liss_download()). New-wave onboarding via onboard_new_wave() extend existing recipe newly released wave.
